title: saponaria thymifolia
intro: Saponaria thymifolia, also known as soapwort or Bouncing Bet, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its white or pink flowers and hairy, oval-shaped leaves. The plant's roots and stems contain saponins, which have been used in herbal medicine for their expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. These medicinal properties have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne. Saponaria thymifolia can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or ointment, and has also been used in traditional herbal remedies to soothe wounds and promote wound healing.
